entries.value: Hi... Sorry, I unfortunately don't have time to explain everything. | Just know that you can move, attack, and dodge, as shown by the drawings on the right and left. | I left you other books... I hope you'll find them... I'd like to... | [The author doesn't seem to have had time to finish the sentence]
Please... Don't come... |Leave here... Run away...
Please... |Don't start over from the beginning... |Don't start over... Everything...,
Don't... Don't cross that door...
I asked you and asked you and asked you...|We... could have found a solution... We still can...|So please...|Please...|Don't start over again and let me...|Take you down...
La-la-la-la-la
I'm... Ahem... | Is it... Is it that... | You heard me singing ?...
Oh my god... This is so embarrassing... | Plus, we don't even know each other... | I don't even know who you are... | But... Wait. We don't even know each other.
Why were you in the Gravity Sanctuary ? | What were you doing there ? | This whole thing isn't very clear... | I'm the one who watches over this sanctuary — this garden belongs to me. | Who are you ?

You say you don't know this place ? That you don't remember anything before waking up in the sanctuary ? | Hmmm... Strange, this memory loss, it's got me curious. | My home is just a few steps from here. | Follow me.
Oh, you're here. My house is on the hill.|(Uh... I hope it's somewhat tidy, I wasn't expecting a visitor...)|Follow me
Here it is, this is my house. Join me inside.
Oh, hey again !|Now then, let’s see… tell me what you remember before you woke up at the sanctuary.
Nothing at all ? It's totally unclear ??? Okaaay.|Do you at least know where we are ?
Alright. Well, it's okay, we'll eventually find something that can help us! Let's not lose hope. | What's your name ?

Do you not even know your own name ??? | Hmm... It's still very strange to wake up like that in such a deeply sacred sanctuary... | Well, I can't just leave you out in the wild like this, poor thing. you'd end up on the wrong path. | Judging by your clothes and muscles, you seem to be a warrior. Let me explain some essential things for survival.

This book traces the history of our world.|It was written by our ancestors, historians.|Even with your amnesia... you know what death is, don’t you ?|It no longer exists. Not since his arrival.|Since that day, no living being has been able to die.|We are condemned to eternity.|You may think it’s a blessing ? It’s not.|The body ages. The suffering, however, persists. Without end.|Death was natural. Without it, balance collapsed.
Look at this book.|This is it: The Square.|It fell from the sky and broke the laws of physics.|Literally.
Four guardians appeared: Electromagnetism, the Weak Interaction, the Strong Interaction... and Gravity.|Each protected a fundamental law.
They tried to stop him.|All failed.|All were corrupted.|All... except one.|The Guardian of Gravity.|He never came down.|Never.|He remains missing.|And he is our only hope.

I've been looking for it since I was born.|My parents gave me this mission before they disappeared.|That's why I watch over this sanctuary.|A forgotten place, abandoned by all... except me.|The others stopped believing.|But I remain.

And you? Why were you in this sanctuary ?|I'm convinced you're searching for it too.|Know this : a Guardian never appears in a sanctuary.|They show up elsewhere, in very specific places.|Rifts. Unstable zones that allow them to cross into our world.|Sanctuaries, on the other hand, are just shelters.|Places of rest, built to welcome them...|They're here to soothe them, to strengthen them.

Anyway, it makes me a bit emotional, sorry for killing the mood.|Look here, this is a life stele.|Every time a creature is supposed to die, it loses what we call SquareCoins and reappears here.|SquareCoins are stones that allow us to become stronger.|The more a creature has, the stronger it is.|You'll see a lot of slimes outside; they're all kinds of living beings who have no more SquareCoins.|Their bodies decomposed into a... gelatinous mass... Kinda gross...|Go ahead, get closer to this stele and activate it.
Perfect ! Now, if by any chance you were defeated by a creature, you'd lose your SquareCoins and reappear here.|But that's not going to happen, right ?
Next to this stele is the forge, where you can upgrade your weapons and armor using SquareCoins and minerals, But you’ll need a pickaxe for that.

Finally, what you see in the back right is a Power Statue. That’s where you can convert your accumulated SquareCoins into strength and skills.|It’s super handy, I’m really lucky to have some at home, even though I don’t really use them.

Many creatures are on the hunt for SquareCoins to grow stronger, and they won’t hesitate to rough you up to take them from you.|You have ten minutes to retrieve your SquareCoins from the spot where you were defeated.| It’s harsh, I know... but during that time, they’re slowly being absorbed by the creature that brought you down.| In the chest near my bed, you’ll find an old sword. Use it to cut through the bushes to the east of here.| And don’t worry, you won’t be alone. Together, we’ll find that god of gravity !

Surprised ? I can transform into a little light to accompany you.| I’ll watch over you, always here to shine the way and help you move forward in this not-so-friendly world.| Let’s be honest, I’m not really made for fighting, which is why I tend to stay in the background.| But with you by my side, I know we’ve got this !| Come on, let’s go collect as many SquareCoins as we can ! You’re going to need all the strength you can get if we want to make it out of here !
Look over there !

See those iron bars ? The lever just behind them probably activates the mechanism to lower them. Classic. But the problem is… how do we even reach it ?|If you’re thinking about heading that way, just know, that’s skeleton territory. I’ve dealt with them before. Not fun.|They’re dangerous and totally power-hungry.| Lately, they’ve been keeping a low profile… too low, if you ask me. Stay sharp. They’d love nothing more than to trap you and drain your strength until you’re just a sad little slime.

Anyway, if I were you, I’d aim for the Village of Tears instead. I’ve never been there myself, but word is, the people there know a thing or two about the god of Gravity. We might find some answers.|Come on, let’s take the Bridge of Tears to the north.

Oh no... The bridge has been destroyed. But why ? Was it a monster, or the villagers themselves ?|Unfortunately, we'll have to go through the skeleton camp to reach the Village of Tears. Is that okay with you ?

Turn back.|There's a Shyron here.|don’t even think about fighting it. It’s a creation of The Square, and its power is beyond anything we’ve ever seen...|Now I understand why the bridge is in ruins, and why the skeletons are keeping their heads down, they’re hiding from this monster.
Look at that ! It seems like you can hit it to trigger some kind of mechanism... Given its hourglass shape, you'd better hurry.

Bunch of bags of bones, why aren't you at your post ?|You know we have to protect ourselves from the great dark monster.|If someone opens the door or if it doesn't work, the whole camp is in danger.|I hope you have good reasons. Otherwise, the king will put you in an absorption cage.|I'm listening to your report.
Yes, chef, we have a good reason !
Yes, chef, actually, someone opened the door !
Yes, chef, actually, someone opened the door !
...|You must be kidding me, right ?|...|GET OUT OF HERE, YOU BUNCH OF INCOMPETENTS, GO WARN THE OTHERS. NOW.
Don't move, little boy.|I knew the person who opened the door would show up.|You're going to pay for this|we're going to put you in an absorption cage.
Eeeek !
BACK OFF !

Mmh... So you see slimes locked up in a prison, and your first thought is to go in ? Seriously ?|That thing is called an absorption cage.|They trap people and creatures in there, and kill them over and over again. Just to drain every last SquareCoin out of them.|And when there's nothing left... when they're reduced to mindless slimes... they toss them out. Or recycle them into who knows what.|Pain ? Suffering ? They couldn’t care less.|It’s just a well-oiled machine. Cold. Disgusting.
Looks like the door only stays open if that button’s pressed.
Oh look, a conveniently placed crate... How original. I don’t need to spell it out for you, right ?
Look at that giant skeleton !|I think it's a mechanical bridge that lets you cross from one platform to another.|There must be a lever or a button somewhere.
Ah, I guess we need another key.|Let's go find it.
Little intruder, things won't go the way you think.|Our king has way more important stuff to do. He's dealing with the big dark monster.|I'll leave you with Bloubi, he'll take real good care of you.
Wizard reporting, my King.|The intruder has entered our dungeon, but with all the traps and mighty warriors we have, he should be stopped quickly.
As for the big black monster... it still hasn’t moved, according to my colleagues.|We really need to close the door again !|Even though we all know you'd make just one bite out of it.
Hi dude.
My King! It’s him! It’s the intruder! The one who opened the door! The one who...
I know. Quiet. Get out of the way.
So... you're the one who opened the door that protected our camp and put all my guys in danger. Is that right ?

Yes, it's us. We're here for two reasons.|First, we're looking for the Guardian of Gravity, and to reach the Village of Tears and gather information, we have to pass through your camp.|Second, we cannot stand by while you cause so much suffering with your methods of torture.|This must stop immediately.

Interesting... A fairy, huh ?| I can’t tell if you’re drowning in Square Coins, or if you’ve got so few that you don’t even have a body left...|And you’re looking for the Guardian of Gravity ? AHAHAH, what a joke.

But seriously... who are you to come here and lecture us ?|Do you have any idea what we’ve been through since The Square appeared ?|My crew and I were crushed by every creature in this world.Until there was no skin left — just bones... one step away from being reduced to Slimes.|This world is cruel. And I won’t let my people get stepped on and suffer forever.|You have to make choices. Yes, we hurt others — but if we hadn’t, they would’ve done it to us. In the end, it’s the same outcome.|Had trouble reaching me ?|No ?|Of course not.|We’re at the bottom of the food chain. Of course we try to protect ourselves.

I understand your perspective. But I reject your judgment.|So no, I’m not just going to open the door and let you stroll into the Village of Tears, not when you’re putting all of us at risk.|Prove to me that you truly want to find this Guardian of Gravity.

Impressive... You know how to fight, kid.|We'll try, and I do mean try, to reconsider our methods to cause less suffering to others.|But know this: I will always put my little men first.|Even if you manage to find the Guardian of Gravity, which I seriously doubt, don't forget that everything you've been through so far was just a warm-up compared to what's ahead.|The creatures that dwell beyond our camp are terrifyingly powerful. Take care of yourself.|Go.
Oh, and I left some gear in a chest behind me.|Good luck to both of you on the rest of your quest.

Well... I must say I understand their point of view, even if I don't condone their methods.|Anyway, well done. I was right to follow you, you fight like a real whirlwind !|But as the king said, after their camp, the creatures become much more dangerous. We'll have to stay on our guard.|Alright, heading to the Village of Tears !
I... I don't want to get killed... I... I'll respawn as a skeleton... I have no SquareCoins... Please... I'm not hurting anyone...|I just wanted... to gather apples for the others...
YOU THERE !
DON’T MOVE IF YOU DON’T WANT TO DIE.|THIS CITY IS OUR ONLY REFUGE AND I’VE NEVER SEEN YOU BEFORE.
No ! Let him through ! He saved me from a pack of wolves while I was out picking apples!|I don’t think he’s an enemy...
...|Alright... If he were a monster greedy for SquareCoins, he would have killed you already.|But we’ve got our eyes on you. You may enter.
I’m the barmaid of the Village of Tears. If you need help or information, come see me when I’m at the tavern, alright ?
Hey again ! Do you need some information ? Or maybe a little pick-me-up ?

Huh ?! You want to try to find the Guardian of Gravity?! But... that sounds so unrealistic...|I’m not trying to discourage you, really !|Try talking to the archivist at the library, she’s passionate about the Guardians.|(She's so passionate about the guardians that it's actually really scary)|She might have some information for you !
Yeees ?... Whaaat does he waaaant ?|Books on botanyyy ?|There are suuuper interesting books about daisies and flowers for making essential oils...|Yaaay....
Did you say Guardian ? Guardian like the Guardians of the fundamental forces ?|Right, is that it ?|Right right, is that it, huh ?
Why do you want to know ?|Tell me everything, I WANT to talk about Guardians,|TELL|ME|WHAT|YOU|WANT
You want to find the Guardian of Gravity ? Is that it ?
*Sniff*|I thought I had found a fan like me, but it’s just a madman... What a disappointment.|Ahh, cruel world... I'm dying...

Are you really serious ? This is beyond madness...|Are you the first living specimen with an IQ below 0 ?|Do you know he will absolutely never show himself ? Even less so when the 3 other guardians are enslaved by The Square ?|Listen to me carefully.

If the Guardian of Gravity shows up and gets beaten by The Square, that would mean The Square would have total control over the 4 fundamental forces, the ones that govern the universe.|In other words, he would literally be God himself.

It’s not that I’m saying you’re weak...|Well, yes I am, you’re clearly useless...|But you’d have to beat the first 3 guardians for the last one to appear. Do you understand ?|Anyway, the path to the Weak Interaction Sanctuary, the least powerful guardian, is sealed and I have no idea how to access it.|But our ancestors speak of a stele hidden in the Desert of Illusion, if you’re really that crazy.|If you find it for me, you’ll have proven you know what you’re doing and I’ll help you. It’s located Southwest from here.
FIRE !

The archivist told us what you were planning to do. | We assume you’re completely insane. But if there’s even the slightest chance you might succeed, we want to seize it at all costs.|You may now go into the desert. Good luck to you, madman.
So this is the Desert of Illusion. Not very welcoming.|I suppose the stele must be in the place that’s protected by this giant force field… But how do we remove this thing ?…

Who are you ? What are you doing here ?|The only thing that could interest you is the stele that opens the seal leading to the Guardian of the Weak Interaction...|She entrusted me, Droxen, with its protection. You shall not pass.|Thanks for your SquareCoins, by the way|they’ll be used to feed my two little mummies...
I guess this button made something move in this dungeon... But I couldn’t tell you what.
Very well... You have made your choice... We both know I won't be able to stop you... | Next time, finish it for good... Promise me that... | Farewell...
Mhhh.... J'ai faim !|Euuuh... T'es qui toi ?

Mmh... How could I avoid making our prisoners suffer, while still "killing" them over and over...|I hope all my boys are doing alright...|Why a fairy ? A fairy is weird... It even looks like a donut...
Careful out there, kids. Try to reach the Village of Tears quickly, even if we hate them around here.

So boring here. I can't wait to travel.|Why cause so much harm ? We can live without inflicting this suffering on others...|I should sharpen my spear someday... But... Mhhh... Can't be bothered.|Is it Thursday? Isn't it swim class today? Oh... But I still can't swim, I sink.|I miss life before The Square... I want to see my family again...

Do you want to enter the skeleton dungeon? Go ahead.|I'm supposed to be the guard, but I'm tired of torturing poor people for a few Square Coins and a bit of strength.|I'm the strongest one here after the king, not because I have the most Square Coins, but because I train every day.|Square Coins offer raw strength, but it's useless if you don't know how to fight, like all those losers around here.|However, if you want to challenge the king, be careful, he completely crushes me—both in Square Coins and technique. Good luck, little boy.
Why do I have to just wait here doing nothing... I was fine at the absorption cages.|I want to see my family again...

A human ??? We're not used to seeing your kind on this side.|Please don't make too much of a mess, times are hard enough as it is.|Oh, you want to reach the Village of Tears ? Sorry, but the king has sealed off the access.|You'll have to go through our dungeon...|...and I'm not sure they'll let you through either.
You're wondering why there are bones and skulls all over the ground when no one's supposed to die ?|Well, don't tell anyone...|...But it's just decoration, it looks pretty.
That Shyron scares me... If he comes closer, I'm out of here right away.|Who's that guy in brown ? He looks really strong...|The barmaid is pretty... I should invite her for a drink... Uh...
Can you not talk to me ? As a soldier, I must stay focused on my objectives.|Have a good day. (...He intimidates me...)
These walls will be absolutely useless against the Shyron.|What happened to all our friends who became skeletons?|I miss my wife...

You know, I once had a wife. The love of my life.|Sadly, she was captured by skeletons during one of their attacks.|The last time I saw her, she was slowly turning into a skeleton, after being killed over and over again.|Unfortunately, they killed me before I could save her. I never found her again...|Sorry to bother you with this. Have a good day.
I always have to guard this door. But why?|I’m bored. I wish I could leave.|Zzzzzz...|
Hi. No, you can’t go through. Yes, have a nice day too.

That soldier who always comes is super weird. I hope he doesn’t invite me or something...|The man who saved me must be really strong... I’m ashamed I ran away like that...|I’d really like to get some apples... But nobody wants to go pick them...
Making weapons and armor. Again. Always.|If only I had some SquareBlocks... We could upgrade the army’s equipment...
Hey... Thanks again for saving me... Sorry I ran away like that...|If I get killed once, I’ll start turning into a skeleton... I don’t have any SquareCoins and... I don’t want to hurt anyone.
I’d love to go into the desert to gather some cactus fruits...|I wonder who that brown-haired boy is... He looks strong.|I hope the Guardian of Weak Interaction will one day unfreeze the lake...

Hello, young man.|Well... I don’t really know if I can call you young... Since our bodies always stay in the same state as long as we don’t die...|The Square arrived 2000 years ago...|Imagine... 2000 years of back pain and arthritis... Sometimes I just want it to end. I’d like to taste heaven... Or hell... At least one of the two, it would still be better.
My back hurts... Every day, I have to take care of these vegetables...|I wish my children and grandchildren were here with me...

I’m a book lover. Unfortunately, in 2000 years, I’ve already read every book in the library hundreds of times.|I don’t remember life before The Square, but it must have been wonderful and so... fleeting...
Guardians... Guardians... Oooh, that excites me...|The guardians must be suuuuuper powerful|Today I dreamed of the guardians... Like every night for 2000 years hehe.
For me, you’ll always be completely crazy. Just so you know.
Where could I find new books?|The archivist is strange, but I like him.|Why go to the tavern when you can read ? It makes no sense.

Hey, did you get to see what the outside of my garden looks like ? | The slimes are nice, aren't they ? | Keep exploring a bit more, as for me, I'll keep researching the guardian of gravity. | Kisses !
Eh toi ! J'aurais besoin d'aide mon ami | Tu vois, il y a une cave là-bas. Visite là et dis moi s'il y a du danger.
Compliqué, hein poto | Mais je crois en toi
T'as réussi ??? | MERCI !
J'ai plus besoin de toi maintenant, merci.
Hey... Psssst... You're not a skeleton, are you ?|Can you kill some slimes and bring me some Slime portions ?|I need them for a project... But if I kill them myself, the king will skin me alive...
Did you manage to get them ?|No ?|Well, I'm not moving from here anyway.
Oh wow ! Awesome ! Here, take this, it's a gift.
I don't need you now.
Thanks a lot for those slime portions, dude !

In this book lies the Tornado Attack, a powerful move that lets you defeat multiple enemies at once by spinning on yourself. | If you move quickly up, right, down, and left, then attack, it will trigger. | Note that the motion must be circular. | You can start from any direction, as long as the input goes clockwise. | This attack is useful when fighting groups of enemies.

In this book lies the Grand Tear attack, a powerful move that lets you unleash a shockwave in front of you, dealing damage to all enemies in your path.|If you hold the attack for at least one second and then release it, the shockwave will crash into all enemies ahead.|It's a useful attack against slow monsters that travel in groups.

In this book lies the Lightning Strike, a powerful move that lets you dash through an enemy and deal a critical hit. | If you start by slashing with your sword, then dodge through the enemy and strike again, you’ll retrace your steps while passing through them, dealing heavy damage. | This attack is especially useful against lone, powerful monsters.
